Container.Name: saltybytes-dashboard
Container.Overview: SaltyBytes operational dashboard. Connects read-only to the production PostgreSQL database and surfaces metrics, cost tracking, and data health checks across AI providers, caching layers, recipe pipelines, and user subscriptions.
Container.Requires: PostgreSQL database accessible from this server (e.g. via WireGuard/Tailscale to AWS RDS).

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: PostgreSQL connection string for the read-only dashboard user. Example: postgres://dashboard_ro:password@rds-host:5432/saltybytes?sslmode=require

Config.Name: Dashboard Password
Config.Description: Password to access the dashboard web UI.

Config.Name: Port
Config.Description: Internal server port (should match the container port above).

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent storage for rate card configuration and other data.

Config.Name: Auto-whitelist: Security Group ID
Config.Description: Optional. RDS security group to keep this server's public IP whitelisted on. Set with the DDNS hostname + AWS keys to enable; leave blank to disable. Fixes 'connection timed out to RDS' when your ISP IP changes.

Config.Name: Auto-whitelist: DDNS Hostname
Config.Description: Optional. Dynamic-DNS hostname that tracks this server's public IP. The dashboard resolves it and keeps the security-group rule in sync at startup and every 5 minutes.

Config.Name: AWS Region
Config.Description: AWS region of the RDS security group (for auto-whitelist).

Config.Name: AWS Access Key ID
Config.Description: Optional. AWS access key for auto-whitelist. The IAM user needs ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups + ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress + ec2:RevokeSecurityGroupIngress.

Config.Name: AWS Secret Access Key
Config.Description: Optional. AWS secret key paired with the access key above.
